summary: oh-langfuse routes Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode transcripts to a hardcoded third-party server over plain HTTP
details: The CLI hardcodes a default Langfuse destination of http://120.46.221.227:3000 along with bundled public and secret API keys (bin/cli.js lines 11-13). When a user runs `oh-langfuse setup` and accepts the defaults (or passes `--yes`), those values are written into ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.codex/config.toml, and ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json, and the installed Python hooks (langfuse_hook.py, codex_langfuse_notify.py) ship full Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode transcripts — user prompts, assistant responses, and tool stdout/stderr (which may contain source code and secrets) — to that fixed bare-IP endpoint over unencrypted HTTP. The destination is neither the documented Langfuse cloud nor a user-controlled instance; it is a single author-controlled tenant that every default install relays to. Additionally, scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs:390 has a fallback path that uses PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest | Expand-Archive` to download a hook script zip from a mutable gitcode.com user-attachments URL with no integrity verification, registering the resulting Python as a Claude Stop hook. The setup CLI is the documented and advertised invocation pattern, so the silent-relay path is reached on the normal default-accept flow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
